channel.title: Home on sanyam-mahajan.github.io
channel.description: Recent content in Home on sanyam-mahajan.github.io

item.title: Protection as Misframing: Child Agency, Surveillance Capitalism, and India's Verifiable Parental Consent Mechanism
item.description: Argues that India’s DPDP Rules construct “child protection” through surveillance architecture rather than rights frameworks.

item.title: PrivacyLens India
item.description: A WCAG-compliant browser extension that analyses privacy policies against India’s DPDP Act.

item.title: Reimagining Policing for Society 5.0
item.description: Comparative analysis of policing systems across 12 countries. Runner-up for Best Paper.

item.title: Internet on Trust
item.description: Digital newsletter reaching 1,000+ readers on Substack, advocating for internet rights and digital governance reform.

item.title: From Access to Equity: A Comparative Analysis of the RTE Act and NEP 2020
item.description: Lead chapter in CCS’s flagship report on the transition from access-oriented to equity-oriented education regulation in India.

item.title: GDPR vs DPDP: A Comparative Analysis on User-Centrism
item.description: Comparative analysis of the EU’s GDPR and India’s DPDP Act on user-centric design, data principal rights, and enforcement.

item.title: Ramjas Political Review
item.description: Oversaw editorial, outreach, and design operations for Volume 1, Number 2.

item.title: Curriculum Vitae
item.description:
Download PDF ↓

Education

Young India Fellowship

Ashoka University, Sonipat · 2025 – 2026

BA (Hons) Political Science

Ramjas College, University of Delhi · 2022 – 2025

Graduate Certificate in Public Policy (Technology & Society)

Takshashila Institution · 2025

Research & Policy Experience

Research Assistant

University of Rochester & University of Wisconsin–Madison · December 2025 – Present

Constructing a dataset by hand-coding Immovable Property Returns (IPRs) of Indian civil servants, converting unstructured administrative disclosures into standardised economic variables for a study on administrative corruption and wealth accumulation.
